scheme_name: Financial Assistance under Odisha Working Journalist Welfare Scheme
page_title: Financial Assistance under Odisha Working Journalist Welfare Scheme

details: The scheme “Odisha Working Journalist Welfare Scheme” was launched by the Information & Public Relations Department, Government of Odisha. Under the Scheme, financial assistance is provided to the legal heir of the working journalist in case of his/her death, and in case of permanent incapacitation. In the event of death of a working journalist, a fixed sum of money may be paid to the widow, and in the event of the deceased not being survived by his widow, equally to the minor children. In the event, the deceased was not married or is not survived by his widow or children, such ex-gratia payment may be sanctioned in favour of his father or mother. The eligible applicant, in such case, can apply online for financial assistance from the Information & Public Relations Department.
benefits: Under the scheme, an ex-gratia of ₹4,00,000/- is provided to the legal heir of the working journalist in case of his/her death and ₹2,00,000/- in case of permanent incapacitation.
eligibility: Journalist should be working in Odisha.
The Journalist should be working for a media house registered with the I&PR department, Odisha.
The applicant should be a legal heir of the working journalist in case of his/her death or in case of permanent incapacitation.
"Permanent Incapacitation" means incapacitation on account of accident, disease, or any other reason which renders the working Journalist wholly unfit for working as such.

online: Visit the official web portal of the Odisha Working Journalist Welfare Scheme Application. i.e.
Click on ‘Apply for Financial Assistance’ found on the bottom of the page.
One new page appears ‘Application for Financial Assistance in case of death of a working journalist’.
The journalist will have to fill up all the mandatory details.
Upon successful form submission One application number is generated which a journalist can use for further reference. i.e. for application status.
The applicant will receive an SMS in respective registered mobile number regarding successful submission of the application.
The applicant can login into the application by giving the respective registered mobile number.
After giving the mobile number, the journalist will receive an OTP on the mobile number. On successful submission of OTP, the applicant will able to login into the application.
On the page, the applicant will be able to view the application status.

documents_required: 4. Copy of accreditation card for accredited journalist
8. Upload the Bank Passbook Front Page

faqs.question: What is the ‘Odisha Working Journalist Welfare Scheme’?
faqs.answer: The ‘Odisha Working Journalist Welfare Scheme’ is an initiative launched by the Information & Public Relations Department, Government of Odisha. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to the legal heir of a working journalist in the event of the journalist's death or permanent incapacitation.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to benefit from the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is designed to provide financial assistance to the legal heirs of working journalists in Odisha who face financial challenges due to the death or permanent incapacitation of the journalist.

faqs.question: What is the ex-gratia amount provided under the scheme in case of a journalist's death?
faqs.answer: In the event of a working journalist's death, the scheme provides an ex-gratia amount of ₹4,00,000/- to the legal heir of the deceased journalist.

faqs.question: How much ex-gratia is offered under the scheme in case of permanent incapacitation of a journalist?
faqs.answer: In case of permanent incapacitation of a working journalist, the Ex-Gratia Scheme provides a financial assistance amounting to ₹2,00,000/- to the legal heir of the deceased journalist.

faqs.question: Who can apply for the benefits in case of the journalist's death or permanent incapacitation?
faqs.answer: The applicant should be a legal heir of the working journalist to claim benefits under the Scheme. In the event of the journalist's death or permanent incapacitation, the legal heir can apply for financial assistance.

faqs.question: How is "Permanent Incapacitation" defined under the scheme?
faqs.answer: "Permanent Incapacitation" refers to a condition where the working journalist becomes wholly unfit for the profession due to accident, disease, or any other reason.

faqs.question: Where can I get more information about the Odisha Working Journalist Welfare Scheme?
faqs.answer: For detailed information, individuals can contact the Information & Public Relations Department of the Government of Odisha.

faqs.question: How to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The eligible applicants need to visit and apply through the official web portal of the “Odisha Working Journalist Welfare Scheme Application”. i.e. https://owjws.odisha.gov.in/
